hindiTranslation: यह देही प्रत्यक्ष नहीं दीखता, यह चिन्तनका विषय नहीं है और यह निर्विकार कहा जाता है ।  अतः इस देहीको ऐसा जानकर शोक नहीं करना चाहिये ॥
englishTranslation: It is unseen, beyond thought, and said to be unchanged. Therefore, knowing it this way, you should not grieve.
essence: What is deepest in you was never available for loss.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The true self is not something you can see, think through, or alter. Knowing that, Arjuna should stop grieving.
simpleInsight: There is relief in seeing that what matters most was never fragile.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna answers his grief with a deeper view of the person within the body. The body changes and dies, but the inner reality is beyond sight, thought, and change, so mourning it makes no sense.
modernRelevance: You reread the message three times after the breakup, trying to make the loss feel logical. Some things cannot be held, explained, or broken in the first place.
translationLiteral: अव्यक्तः (unseen) / अयम् (this) / अचिन्त्यः (unthinkable) / अयम् (this) / अविकार्यः (unchanging) / अयम् (this) / उच्यते (is said to be) / तस्मात् (therefore) / एवम् (thus) / विदित्वा (having known) / एनम् (this) / न (not) / अनुशोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (you should)
insight: It shifts the mind from identifying with what changes to resting in what does not.

quoteVariants.text: What you are cannot be seen, thought, or changed. Grief has no place there.
The one you fear losing was never graspable.
